An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
Proof that God is one and not many.

metadata.author: John of Damascus: Exposition of the Orthodox Faith

ccel.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

title: Proof that God is one and not many.

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: We have, then, adequately demonstrated that there is a God, and that His essence is incomprehensible.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But that God is one and not many is no matter of doubt to those who believe in the Holy Scriptures.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For the Lord says in the beginning of the Law: I am the Lord thy God, which have brought thee out of the land of Egypt.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Thou shalt have no other Gods before Me.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And again He says, Hear, O Israel, the Lord our God is one Lord.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And in Isaiah the prophet we read, For I am the first God and I am the last, and beside Me there is no God.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Before Me there was not any God, nor after Me will there be any God, and beside Me there is no God.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And the Lord, too, in the holy gospels speaketh these words to His Father, And this is life eternal, that they may know Thee the only true God.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But with those that do not believe in the Holy Scriptures we will reason thus.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The Deity is perfect, and without blemish in goodness, and wisdom, and power, without beginning, without end, everlasting, uncircumscribed, and in short, perfect in all things.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Should we say, then, that there are many Gods, we must recognise difference among the many.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For if there is no difference among them, they are one rather than many.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But if there is difference among them, what becomes of the perfectness?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For that which comes short of perfection, whether it be in goodness, or power, or wisdom, or time, or place, could not be God.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But it is this very identity in all respects that shews that the Deity is one and not many.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Again, if there are many Gods, how can one maintain that God is uncircumscribed?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For where the one would be, the other could not be.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Further, how could the world be governed by many and saved from dissolution and destruction, while strife is seen to rage between the rulers?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For difference introduces strife.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And if any one should say that each rules over a part, what of that which established this order and gave to each his particular realm?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For this would the rather be God.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Therefore, God is one, perfect, uncircumscribed, maker of the universe, and its preserver and governor, exceeding and preceding all perfection.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Moreover, it is a natural necessity that duality should originate in unity.
